dialogueChunks.intro: The caravan was hit, {player_name}. The {enemy_type} attacked at the narrowest point of the road. The volunteers are holding but they can't last.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: The baker's mother took an arrow in the shoulder and is still driving the lead wagon with one hand. She won't stop. {quest_ally} managed to pull one wagon behind cover, but {quest_item} was scattered across the road when the second wagon overturned. {target_npc} is trying to rally the survivors.
dialogueChunks.outro: If we lose {quest_item}, the trip was for nothing and those people bled for nothing. Help {target_npc} recover the supplies and drive back the {enemy_type}.

playerResponses.text: Nobody's bleeding for nothing today. Let's move.
We should cut our losses and save the people.
The caravan is lost. We need to retreat.

dialogueChunks.intro: We got the refugees to the halfway point, but scouts report a second {enemy_type} war band approaching from {location_hint}. The elder's diversion has been discovered.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: {target_npc} wants to split the column: send the fastest ahead to {location} while the rest shelter in a defensible position. But splitting means someone has to stay with the slower group and face whatever comes. {quest_ally} has already volunteered. Said they've lived long enough.
dialogueChunks.outro: We need {quest_item} to fortify the shelter point. If {quest_ally} is going to hold the line for {quest_stakes}, at least give them a fighting chance.

playerResponses.text: I'll fortify the position. Nobody else needs to die today.
The whole column should stay together. Splitting is a death sentence.

dialogueChunks.intro: A new crack opened in {quest_focus} an hour ago. Water is pouring through faster than we can patch it. The mason is waist-deep and refusing to come out.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: {target_npc} tried to pull him back by force. He shoved them away and said there are children in the houses below. He's mixing mortar with shaking hands while the river pushes against his chest. We need more {quest_item} to reinforce the breach from the upstream side.
dialogueChunks.outro: The {quest_threat} could take him any moment. But if that wall fails, {quest_stakes} lose everything. Find {quest_item} {location_hint} and bring it back before the next surge hits.

playerResponses.text: I'll get the materials. Keep that man alive until I'm back.
Pull him out. No dam is worth a life.
The dam is going to fail regardless. Start evacuating.
